original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: American Academy of Otolaryngology–Head and Neck Surgery Foundation
original_registry_entry.exact_document_title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Sudden Hearing Loss (Update)
original_registry_entry.jurisdiction: United States specialty guideline; international evidence requiring UAE ENT and audiology adaptation
original_registry_entry.population: Adults aged 18 years and older presenting with sudden hearing loss, primarily idiopathic sudden sensorineural hearing loss.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Initial and follow-up assessment by clinicians who diagnose or manage adult sudden hearing loss.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Exact for documenting sudden onset, laterality, associated neurological or otological context, examination, audiometric confirmation, reviewed results, and follow-up. It does not infer sensorineural loss or generate treatment, imaging, referral, timing, or urgency.

exact_sections.heading: Key action statements 1–6 — initial distinction, modifying factors, and audiometric confirmation
exact_sections.locator: official guideline page, executive summary, and fact sheet key action statements 1–6
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting sudden onset, bilateral or recurrent episodes, focal neurological findings, clinician-performed ear assessment, and clinician-reviewed audiometry without asserting a diagnosis or ordering a test.

exact_sections.heading: Key action statements 12–13 — education, follow-up, and outcomes
exact_sections.locator: official guideline page and fact sheet key action statements 12–13
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports recording clinician-completed information discussion, questions, follow-up assessment, and reviewed hearing outcomes without generating treatment or a follow-up interval.

title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Sudden Hearing Loss (Update)
publisher: American Academy of Otolaryngology–Head and Neck Surgery Foundation
jurisdiction: United States specialty guideline; international evidence requiring UAE ENT and audiology adaptation
population: Adults aged 18 years and older presenting with sudden hearing loss, primarily idiopathic sudden sensorineural hearing loss.
intended_setting: Initial and follow-up assessment by clinicians who diagnose or manage adult sudden hearing loss.
